page_content:
Prerequisite: Minimum of C- in NFSC100, BCHM462 and BSC1440.

NFSC450 Food and Nutrient Analysis (3 Credits)

Methods and practices of the analysis of foods and nutrients. An overview of the principles and basic mechanisms used in many of the analytical procedures commonly used in food and nutrition research. Emphasis will be placed on hands-on development of skills necessary to complete each analytical procedure; and on the accurate and concise description of the methodology and results from their application and on the regulations governing food analysis for nutritional labeling. Prerequisite: BCHM461 and NFSC100.
